content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Sitemap Index Graph model · Theory Sitemap Index Graph, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 7611 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to stream bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance admission; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Sitemap Index Graph model · Theory Sitemap Index Graph, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Sitemap index files cannot form cycles.
